{{Quote|I'm not here to surrender. I am here to take possession of Bastion in the name of the Imperial Throne. My Throne.|[[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]|<ref name="Legacy 3" />}}
The '''Fall of Bastion''' was an event that took place in [[137 ABY]] in which [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] and his [[Empire-in-exile|followers]] took [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] back from [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|loyalists]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|The true emperor, Darth Krayt, has ordered your arrest.|Lieutenant Kiefer, to Roan Fel|<ref name="Legacy 3" />}}
In [[130 ABY]], [[Darth Krayt]], the leader of the [[One Sith]], had taken the [[Fel Empire|Imperial]] throne from [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] following the [[Sith–Imperial War]], where the [[Fel Empire|Empire]] retook [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], which was the leading government at the time. Krayt had tried to kill Fel during this hostile takeover, but Fel had replaced himself with a [[Roan Fel's double|double]]. Fel became a thorn in Krayt's side, leading guerrilla attacks, similar to what the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] had done almost [[0 BBY/Legends|140 years ago]]. Fel could not lead a proper offensive without a major base, so he moved to take [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], the former capital of the [[Imperial Remnant]], the government that preceded Fel's Empire.<ref name="Legacy 3" />

==Changing loyalties==
{{Quote|I call upon you, my loyal stormtroopers, to join with me and take back our Empire which you have so long and faithfully served.|Roan Fel arrival on Bastion restored the Fel Empire|<ref name="Legacy 3" />}}
Emperor [[Roan Fel]] arrived at the planet aboard a [[Nune-class Imperial shuttle|''Nune''-class Imperial Shuttle]], escorted by three of his [[Imperial Knight]]s, Masters [[Antares Draco]], [[Ganner Krieg]], and [[Sigel Dare]]. He was received by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Kiefer]] of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]], who ordered him to surrender. However, Fel gave an impassioned speech to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], calling upon them to join him in the fight against the usurper [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. The 501st immediately knelt before their rightful Emperor, thus leaving the service of Krayt's Empire.<ref name="Legacy 3" />

Lieutenant Kiefer ordered the stormtroopers to stand, stating that they owed no loyalty to the deposed Emperor. When none of them obeyed him, Kiefer drew his blaster on Fel, intending to execute him. However, Kiefer was quickly shot down under the orders of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Oron Jaeger]], commanding officer of the 501st Legion. Jaeger told Fel that his forces were under Fel's command, and the takeover was complete.<ref name="Legacy 3" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Welcome back sire. Welcome home.|Oron Jaeger, to Roan Fel|<ref name="Legacy 3" />}}
With Bastion under the Empire-in-exile control, Fel moved to gather his forces against Krayt, hoping to keep his quiet takeover of Bastion a secret to the galaxy, especially to Krayt. However, Krayt, hoping to draw Fel into the open, ordered his [[Hand|personal assassin]], [[Darth Talon]], to capture or drive Fel's daughter, [[Marasiah Fel]], to her father.<ref name="Legacy 3" /> Though his daughter was on the run, Fel ordered his Imperial Knights, especially Draco and Krieg, to keep away from Marasiah. Fel knew that Krayt couldn't find out about Bastion's capture in order for Fel to secretly gather his forces, and if Draco and Krieg rescued Marasiah, Imperial forces would track Draco and Krieg back to Bastion, exposing Fel. Despite this, Draco and Krieg disobeyed Fel, and went to rescue Marasiah on [[Vendaxa/Legends|Vendaxa]], where she and [[Astraal Vao]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] from the [[Imperial Mission]], were trapped by Talon.<ref name="Legacy 6">[[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]]</ref> Krieg and Draco were able to save them from the Sith with help from [[Cade Skywalker]], the last heir of the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]]. However, the mission revealed Fel's presence on Bastion.<ref name="Legacy 7">[[Legacy (2006) 7|''Legacy'' (2006) 7]]</ref> This allowed Krayt to send [[Darth Kruhl]] on a mission to [[Duel on Bastion|assassinate]] Fel, but failed.<ref name="Legacy 13">[[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Fall of Bastion takes place during the [[Legacy (2006) 3|third issue]] of ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'', during the [[Star Wars: Legacy: Broken|Broken]] story arc. It serves as an advancement of the plot, and gives a reason why Draco and Krieg are not allowed to save Marasiah.
